title: chaenomeles speciosa f. extuscoccinea
intro: Chaenomeles speciosa f. pleniflora, commonly known as the double-flowered flowering quince, is a type of flowering shrub belonging to the rose family. It is known for its vibrant pink flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. Chaenomeles speciosa f. pleniflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
